metric_value: Unisys positions as 'one-stop-shop': hardware + OS + middleware + integration services to relieve MIS complexity burden
notes: Core Unisys differentiator vs best-of-breed open systems approach

metric_value: Unisys reorganized 1995 into 3 groups: Global Customer Service Group, Information Service Group, Computer Systems Group
notes: Computer Systems Group includes Enterprise Server Division responsible for ClearPath SMP

metric_value: Since 1994 Aberdeen profile, Unisys mission evolved from systems-centric to solution-centric enterprise computing
notes: Aberdeen tracks evolution from prior 1994 profile

metric_value: SMP5200 (workgroup): April 1996, 1-2 processors, 100/133 MHz; SMP5400 (departmental): April 1996, up to 4 processors; SMP6400: H2 1996, 166/200 MHz Pentium Pro
notes: Complete 1996 server launch schedule from Aberdeen Profile

metric_value: SMP5200: 256 MB memory, 24 GB disk capacity; suitable for resource-hungry workgroup applications
notes: Maximum configuration at launch

metric_value: SMP61000: up to 10 processors at 150 MHz (200 MHz Pentium Pro in Q3); up to 4 GB RAM initially, 8 GB by year-end; 7 PCI + 5 EISA slots
notes: SMP61000 replacing U6000/550 and /580 series

metric_value: SCM (Synchronous Coherent Memory) passive backplane: 64-bit, 533 MBps; up to 24 processors expected by year-end upgrade
notes: Proprietary architecture enabling processor board mixing at different speeds

metric_value: SMP5400/6400: dual PCI bus, large memory, internal RAID (0,1,5); SMP61000: 6 hot-swappable disks, redundant power supplies
notes: RAS (Reliability, Availability, Security) features throughout product line

metric_name: Unisys OS transition: SVR4/MP to UnixWare
metric_value: Unisys transitioning from proprietary SVR4/MP Unix to SCO-consortium UnixWare 2.1 across server line in 1996
notes: OS transition signals Unisys commitment to open standards Unix

metric_value: OPUS: 8-128 Pentium processors; SVR4 Unix with Chorus microkernel; developed with Intel; suited for highest-performance requirements
notes: Unisys's MPP solution at the extreme high end; Intel co-development

metric_value: Veritas Vx Reliant: cluster 2-4 SMP nodes; 99.9% to 99.999% uptime; optional distributed lock manager for Oracle Parallel Server
notes: Based on Pyramid Technology Reliant software; failure handling between 4 nodes

metric_value: Move to open systems shifted responsibility for component integration from suppliers to users; MIS overwhelmed by hundreds of components requiring integration expertise
notes: Aberdeen's framing of the core market need Unisys addresses

metric_name: Unisys ISG as internal systems integrator advantage
metric_value: Unisys Information Services Group (ISG) serves as in-house systems integrator; provides tight supplier ecosystem control unavailable to pure hardware vendors
notes: Key competitive differentiator vs. HP, Compaq in enterprise server market

metric_value: Aberdeen expects Unisys to enhance SCM bus by year-end to support 24 processors in SMP61000
notes: Forward-looking Aberdeen prediction within the study period

metric_value: Desktop, mid-tier servers, and back-end systems hardware requiring enterprise-wide integration
notes: Factor 1 of Aberdeen's enterprise complexity framework

metric_value: Desktop, mid-tier, and back-end operating systems requiring consistent administration
notes: Factor 2 of enterprise complexity framework

metric_value: Protocols, devices, network operating systems — internal and external links requiring unified management
notes: Factor 3 of enterprise complexity framework

metric_value: Reliability, Availability, Security (RAS): old data center standard now critical as applications shift from 'nice to have' to 'must have'
notes: Factor 4; RAS cited as overriding requirement driving full-service supplier demand

metric_value: Aberdeen: Unisys mission to continue moving from systems-centric to solution-centric; ClearPath SMP line to mature as enterprise platform
notes: Pairs with OBS-020

metric_value: Unisys ClearPath Forward platform survived and continues with roadmap to 2050; however Unisys exited commodity Intel server market and refocused on services and ClearPath legacy platforms
notes: Partial confirmation: ClearPath survived but Unisys's broader server market share eroded; services-led transformation succeeded

metric_value: SMP61000: 6 hot-swappable internal disks (RAID 0/1/5); additional modular cabinets for 7+ more hot-swappable drives; enterprise-grade I/O
notes: Table 1 - SMP System Features referenced in study

metric_value: Unisys is the largest reseller of Oracle; strong RDBMS relationships including Oracle, Informix, Red Brick
notes: Market position data from Aberdeen's Unisys assessment
